key_insight: The paper proposes a reusable, multi-level ontology architecture that separates abstraction level and consumer audience, while embedding a seven-tier mechanistic explanation structure for materials knowledge.
engineering_relevance: This enables materials ontologies to support both scientific reasoning and regulatory digital product passport needs without rebuilding a new ontology for each application domain.
method: Define four abstraction levels: L0 bridges, L1 material-agnostic lab notebook, L2 material-class-specific, L3 categorical reasoning.
Use two audience tracks: material users and compliance users.
Organize L2 with seven mechanistic tiers: Symmetry, Energy/DFT, Thermo/CALPHAD, Kinetics, Microstructure, Defect chemistry, Bonding.
evidence_signal: The reference implementation, OntoCrafter Ceramics Ontology (OCO v0.94), reports 5,196 classes, 167,348 OWL axioms, 829 bridge mappings, 1,172 SHACL shapes, and 163 competency questions.
watch_item: The architecture is promising, but its practical value will depend on how consistently the modular levels and bridge mappings can be maintained across many materials domains and evolving regulations.
paper_title: A Multi-Level Architecture for Reusable Materials Ontologies -- The OntoCrafter Ceramics Ontology (OCO) as Reference Implementation
